items.summary: Nvidia's Q1 FY2027 earnings report on May 20 after market close is expected to significantly impact the stock market on May 21. As the AI chip leader, Nvidia's guidance on demand, inference growth, China export progress, and AI's interaction with other technologies will provide crucial visibility into the broader AI sector and tech stocks. The company forecasts 77% revenue growth to $78 billion with 74% gross margins.

items.title: 2 AI Stocks to Avoid (Including BigBear.ai) and 1 to Buy Now
items.summary: BigBear.ai and C3.ai are losing relevance in the crowded AI software market due to declining revenues, widening losses, and intense competition. BigBear.ai's revenue declined from $146M to $128M (2021-2025) with net losses expanding to $294M, while C3.ai faces projected revenue decline from $389M to $251M (fiscal 2025-2028). In contrast, Broadcom is recommended as a superior AI play, with AI chip sales surging 65% to $20B in fiscal 2025 and projected to reach $60-90B by fiscal 2027, trading at a reasonable 18x next year's adjusted EBITDA.

items.title: Under Greg Abel, Apple Stock Looks Like It's Here to Stay in Berkshire Hathaway's Portfolio
items.summary: Under new CEO Greg Abel, Berkshire Hathaway halted its multi-quarter Apple selling trend and more than tripled its Alphabet stake in Q1 2026, signaling a shift toward technology investments. Apple reported strong fundamentals with 17% revenue growth and 22% EPS growth, though rising memory costs present a near-term risk.

items.title: Brookfield Corporation Just Reported. Here Are 3 Things Investors Need to Know.
items.summary: Brookfield Corporation reported strong Q1 results and CEO Bruce Flatt emphasized the importance of focusing on long-term value creation over short-term market noise. The company is capitalizing on three major megatrends: digitalization, decarbonization, and deglobalization, with new opportunities emerging from AI infrastructure demand. Flatt also addressed private credit concerns, arguing that Brookfield's disciplined approach focused on real asset-backed lending insulates it from potential disruptions while positioning it to benefit from AI infrastructure growth.

items.title: 3 Monster Energy Stocks to Hold for the Next 10 Years
items.summary: The article recommends three energy stocks for long-term 10-year investment: Chevron, Williams Companies, and Brookfield Renewable. Chevron offers diversified upstream, midstream, and downstream operations with 39 years of consecutive dividend increases and expected 23% EPS CAGR through 2028. Williams Companies operates 33,000 miles of natural gas pipelines and benefits from AI data center demand growth with 11% EBITDA CAGR expected. Brookfield Renewable provides green energy solutions with 47 GW of operating capacity and 200 GW in pipeline, profiting from AI infrastructure and decarbonization trends.

items.title: Prediction: Here's What a $5,000 Investment in NuScale Power (SMR) Stock Will Be Worth in 10 Years
items.summary: NuScale Power, a small modular reactor (SMR) developer, has significant growth potential driven by rising demand for nuclear energy from AI data centers. The company is the first to receive NRC design approval and is planning major projects including the largest U.S. nuclear deployment program with the Tennessee Valley Authority. However, it currently posts losses, has yet to build reactors, and faces competition. A $5,000 investment could potentially multiply 10x by 2036 if the company succeeds and scales revenue, but risks include delays, cash crunches, and share dilution.

items.title: OpenAI Merges ChatGPT And Codex, Taps Greg Brockman To Lead Product Strategy Ahead Of Potential IPO: Report
items.summary: OpenAI is consolidating its core products—ChatGPT, Codex AI coding agent, and developer API—under a unified product structure ahead of a potential IPO filing later this year. Greg Brockman has been given permanent responsibility for product strategy, while Thibault Sottiaux will lead the merged core product team. The restructuring follows the departure of several senior executives and positions OpenAI to compete more effectively against rivals Anthropic and Alphabet in AI markets.

items.title: Nvidia vs. Alphabet: Which Will Be the World's Biggest Company By the End of 2026?
items.summary: Nvidia currently holds the title of world's largest company with a $5.5 trillion market cap, but Alphabet is closing in at $4.8 trillion and has significantly outperformed Nvidia over the past year. Alphabet's cloud division is experiencing explosive growth with 63% revenue increase and 203% operating income growth, driven by AI demand. However, Nvidia benefits from this cloud expansion as it supplies the AI chips powering these services. Both companies are positioned as excellent long-term investments with strong competitive advantages in AI and cloud computing.

items.title: NextEra Energy, Dominion In Talks To Create $400 Billion Utility Giant As Electricity Demand Surges Amid AI Boom: Report
items.summary: NextEra Energy and Dominion Energy are in talks to merge in a mostly stock-based transaction that would create a roughly $400 billion utility powerhouse. The merger would combine two of the nation's largest electricity providers to meet surging power demand from AI data centers, industrial reshoring, and electrification. Dominion's footprint in Virginia and the Carolinas would expand NextEra's reach into Northern Virginia's data center hub.

items.title: Axe Compute Inc. Reports First Quarter 2026 Financial Results and Provides Business Update
items.summary: Axe Compute Inc. (NASDAQ: AGPU) announced a $260 million 36-month enterprise contract for dedicated GPU compute capacity, representing approximately $21 million in quarterly revenue once deployed in Q3 2026. The company also appointed Kyle Okamoto as President and Jeremy Yaukey-Witter as CFO. Q1 2026 saw first compute services revenue recognition with a $645k contract liability from customer prepayments, though the company reported a net loss of $7.7 million primarily due to $4.3 million in mark-to-market losses on digital asset holdings.

items.title: Warren Buffett's Successor, Greg Abel, Dumped Amazon and Domino's, and More Than Tripled Berkshire's Stake in a Virtual Monopoly in a Massive Portfolio Overhaul
items.summary: Greg Abel, who took over as CEO of Berkshire Hathaway on December 31, executed a major portfolio overhaul in Q1 2026. He completely exited 16 positions including Amazon, Domino's Pizza, Visa, Mastercard, and UnitedHealth Group, while more than tripling Berkshire's stake in Alphabet to approximately $23 billion. The moves signal a shift toward tech investments and fundamental bargains, marking a departure from Warren Buffett's traditional investment approach.

items.title: SalesCloser Deepens Conversational AI Moat with Dedicated GPU Inference Cluster, Enabling Custom Model Fine-Tuning, Agentic Workflows, and Regulated-Industry Readiness
items.summary: SalesCloser has commissioned a dedicated AI inference cluster using NVIDIA Blackwell-class GPUs hosted on Canadian renewable energy infrastructure. The owned inference layer enables custom model fine-tuning, sophisticated agentic workflows, data sovereignty for regulated industries, reduced vendor dependency, and sustainable operations—positioning the company for enterprise market expansion.

items.title: AI/R Compass UOL avança na aplicação de inteligência artificial em DevOps e conquista reconhecimento da Amazon Web Services
items.summary: AI/R Compass UOL, a subsidiary of AI/R specializing in Agentic AI, has achieved AWS DevOps Competency recognition from Amazon Web Services. The company integrates advanced automation, continuous monitoring, and security practices into its DevOps model. Recent projects demonstrate significant results: a financial sector client achieved 25% operational cost reduction, while a sports industry client built a scalable platform serving over 500,000 users.

items.title: Arm Holdings CEO Rene Haas Has a Big Warning for Intel and AMD
items.summary: Arm Holdings is entering the server CPU market with its new AGI CPU, claiming it can reduce AI data center capital costs by up to $10 billion per gigawatt with over 2x performance per rack compared to x86 platforms. While Arm has secured major customers like Meta and expects $15 billion in annual revenue by 2031, AMD and Intel currently dominate with significantly larger data center revenues ($5.8B and $5.1B respectively in Q1). The market is large enough for multiple players, but Arm's energy-efficient architecture poses a competitive threat to the x86 incumbents.

items.title: Nvidia Stock Sank Today. Is It a Buy With Earnings Due Next Week?
items.summary: Nvidia shares dropped 3.3% today, underperforming the broader market decline, after CEO Jensen Huang's China visit yielded no major chip orders. Chinese competitors Tencent and Alibaba are increasing domestic chip production, potentially limiting Nvidia's China expansion. However, with earnings due May 20 and the company's history of beating guidance, today's dip could present a buying opportunity.

items.title: SpaceX IPO Prospectus Could Reportedly Drop Next Week—Here's A Peek Into What Investors May Be Buying At Astronomical Valuations
items.summary: SpaceX is preparing for a historic IPO targeting $70-75 billion in funding at a $1.75 trillion valuation. While Starlink's satellite internet business shows strong growth with 50% YoY revenue increase and 63% EBITDA margins, xAI's $9.5 billion cash burn in 2025 resulted in a $5 billion net loss. Governance concerns include Elon Musk's 79% voting control despite 42% equity stake. The prospectus is expected this week, with potential index inclusion driving significant passive buying pressure.

items.title: 3 AI Data Center Stocks Worth Watching for Capital Rotation
items.summary: As semiconductor and memory chip stocks reach elevated valuations, capital is expected to rotate into AI data center infrastructure. Three companies are highlighted: Applied Digital (APLD) with $23 billion in contracted revenue and 139% YoY growth; Iren (IREN) with a $9.7 billion Microsoft deal and $3.4 billion NVIDIA contract; and CoreWeave (CRWV), the largest with $5.13 billion in annual revenue growing at triple-digit rates.

items.title: Meta vs. Snap: What Do Their Quarterly Revenue Trends Tell Investors?
items.summary: Meta and Snap show contrasting revenue trajectories, with Meta reporting 33% year-over-year Q1 revenue growth to $56.3 billion versus Snap's modest 12% growth to $1.5 billion. Meta's profitability (48% net margin) and successful AI investments in hardware stand in stark contrast to Snap's unprofitability (-6% net margin), making Meta a more appealing investment despite both companies operating in the social media advertising space.

items.title: Meta's AI Chief Rejects 'Incorrect' Claims Of Mark Zuckerberg's Big-Money Talent Poaching: 'People Joined Because...'
items.summary: Meta's AI Chief Alexandr Wang dismissed claims that the company aggressively recruited top AI researchers with massive financial incentives, stating that researchers were attracted by access to high computing power and individualized recruiting processes rather than money. The article highlights Meta's intense competition for AI talent, including reports of CEO Mark Zuckerberg personally recruiting from OpenAI and Meta spending approximately $10 billion annually on AI talent.

items.title: Tristan Thompson Reveals How NBA Dinners Around Warriors Games Landed Him An Anthropic Investment
items.summary: NBA champion Tristan Thompson disclosed he holds equity in Anthropic (valued at ~$900 billion) through an investment made during Silicon Valley dinners around Warriors games. Thompson invested via a special purpose vehicle (SPV) pooling money from fellow athletes. The investment highlights how access to pre-IPO AI companies largely depends on insider networks, though secondary markets have emerged. Anthropic is expected to go public in 2026.

items.title: Prediction: Nvidia Will Deliver Another Blowout Earnings Report on May 20, but It Won't Move the Stock in a Big Way
items.summary: Nvidia is expected to report strong fiscal Q1 2027 earnings on May 20 with revenue growth of ~44% YoY and EPS growth of ~115% YoY, driven by continued AI infrastructure spending from major hyperscalers. However, the article predicts the stock won't see significant near-term gains due to the company's massive market cap requiring exceptional beats to move the stock, and because institutional investors likely already price in strong results.

items.title: ROSEN, A GLOBAL AND LEADING LAW FIRM, Encourages Babcock & Wilcox Enterprises, Inc. Investors to Secure Counsel Before Important Deadline in Securities Class Action - BW
items.summary: A securities class action lawsuit has been filed against Babcock & Wilcox Enterprises (BW) for allegedly making false and misleading statements regarding a Power Generation Contract. The lawsuit claims that B&W's largest shareholder BRC Group Holdings stood on both sides of the contract and that Applied Digital did not actually need the products/services, raising questions about revenue recognition and overstating business prospects. Investors who purchased BW securities between November 5, 2025 and March 11, 2026 may be entitled to compensation, with a June 15, 2026 deadline to serve as lead plaintiff.

items.title: 3 Top Stocks That Growth Investors Should Buy Now
items.summary: The article recommends three growth stocks for investors: Nvidia, Meta Platforms, and Amazon. Nvidia is highlighted for its dominant position in AI chip manufacturing with expected 72% revenue growth and massive data center opportunities through 2030. Meta is valued cheaply by price-to-operating-cash-flow metrics despite heavy AI investments and strong 33% revenue growth. Amazon is recommended primarily for its AWS cloud division, which generates 59% of operating profits while representing only 21% of revenue, with 28% year-over-year growth.

items.title: Cerebras CEO Warns US Chip Manufacturing Catch-Up Could Take 15 Years
items.summary: Cerebras Systems went public on Nasdaq at $185 per share, with shares surging 90.28% on strong AI investor enthusiasm. CEO Andrew Feldman stated the company's custom silicon chips can process AI models faster than competitors like Nvidia. However, Feldman warned that US efforts to expand advanced semiconductor manufacturing could take 10-15 years due to supply chain complexity and massive capital requirements. The company relies on Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing for chip production.

items.title: Down 66%, Should You Buy the Dip in Rigetti Computing?
items.summary: Rigetti Computing stock has plummeted 66% from all-time highs after a speculative surge in 2025. Despite the pullback, the company's valuation remains unjustified with only $7.1 million in 2025 revenue against $216 million in net losses. With a P/S ratio of 607, structural operating losses of ~$100 million annually, and insider selling, the article warns investors that this falling stock may not represent a buying opportunity without significant commercial breakthroughs.

items.title: Dow Jones Hits 50,000 For the First Time As Nvidia And Broadcom Power Market Indexes Higher
items.summary: The Dow Jones Industrial Average crossed 50,000 for the first time ever on May 14, 2026, with all three major indexes gaining approximately 1%. AI chip stocks, particularly Nvidia and Broadcom, drove the market higher following regulatory approval for Nvidia to sell H200 accelerators in China. Cisco jumped 17% on earnings, while Boeing declined 4.5% despite positive trade news.

items.title: Dow Jones Rally Looks Fragile With Inflation Data Running Hot
items.summary: The Dow Jones climbed back above 50,000 driven by Cisco's strong earnings beat and guidance raise, while Nvidia gained on news of Chinese H200 chip approvals. However, the rally appears fragile as inflation data runs hot with import prices spiking 1.9% and energy costs surging, offsetting positive consumer resilience metrics. The Trump-Xi summit in Beijing provided mild sentiment support but delivered limited concrete deliverables.

items.title: Better Datacenter Stock: CoreWeave (CRWV) or Nebius (NBIS)?
items.summary: CoreWeave and Nebius are both AI infrastructure companies experiencing rapid growth, but the article recommends Nebius as the better buy. CoreWeave has impressive revenue growth but faces widening losses and a high debt-to-equity ratio of 10.7. Nebius shows stronger growth prospects, lower debt (2.1 ratio), and is expected to achieve profitability by 2028, despite trading at a higher valuation multiple.

items.title: Ideal Power Reports First Quarter 2026 Financial Results
items.summary: Ideal Power (NASDAQ:IPWR) reported Q1 2026 results with no commercial revenue, a net loss of $3.6 million, and cash of $16.4 million. The company advanced its B-TRAN® semiconductor technology with multiple new projects including partnerships with an Asia customer for solid-state circuit breakers, a letter of intent with an industry partner for NVIDIA's 800V AI data center architecture, and continued progress with Stellantis on EV applications.

items.title: Stock Market Today: S&P 500, Dow, Nasdaq Futures Gain As Trump Clears NVDA's H200 Sales To China— Cisco Systems, Doximity In Focus (UPDATED)
items.summary: U.S. stock futures rose on Thursday as President Trump addresses trade issues with China. Cisco Systems surged 18.8% on better-than-expected Q3 results and raised FY26 guidance. Doximity plunged 21.46% on mixed Q4 results and weak guidance. Grocery Outlet gained 16.41% after beating EPS estimates by 150%. The Fed is expected to hold rates steady in June with 98.6% probability. Nvidia's H200 chip sales to Chinese firms were cleared by the U.S., though Beijing halted deliveries.

items.title: Nasdaq 100 Reaches Record Highs as Nvidia Rises
items.summary: US stocks reached record highs on Thursday with the Nasdaq and S&P 500 driven by technology and chipmaker gains. Nvidia rose after reports that the US approved Chinese firms to purchase its H200 AI chip, easing trade restrictions. Cisco surged 18% following an AI-focused restructuring plan with 4,000 job cuts but strong revenue growth forecasts. Markets brushed aside inflation concerns as investors monitored the US-China summit and awaited retail sales data.

items.title: Wall Street Wants to See $78.8 Billion in Revenues from Nvidia on May 20. Can It Pass the Test?
items.summary: Nvidia is expected to report $78.8 billion in Q1 fiscal 2027 revenue (77% YoY growth) on May 20, 2026. While the company has consistently beaten expectations, investors will closely watch forward guidance for Q2 (expected $86.6B) and gross margins (targeted at 75%). The four largest customers—Amazon, Microsoft, Alphabet, and Meta—are collectively spending over $700 billion on AI infrastructure this year, supporting Nvidia's growth, though the author questions whether AI spending has gotten ahead of actual returns.

items.title: Here's When Tesla's Robotaxi Rollout Will Really Ramp Up
items.summary: Tesla is aggressively expanding its robotaxi fleet, currently operating 39 unsupervised robotaxis across Austin, Dallas, and Houston. CEO Elon Musk clarified that the company will wait for Full Self-Driving v15 software release (expected early 2027) before launching a large-scale rollout, as major architectural improvements are needed to enhance safety. The company is preparing for expansion into Phoenix, Miami, Orlando, Tampa, and Las Vegas in the meantime.

items.title: Micron Technology: The AI Platform Moat Wall Street Has Yet to Fully Price
items.summary: Micron Technology has transformed from a cyclical memory supplier into a strategic AI infrastructure platform partner, with stock gains of 700%+ in 12 months. Despite impressive Q2 FY2026 results (revenue $23.86B, non-GAAP EPS $12.20) and strong Q3 guidance, the stock trades at a depressed 7.3x forward P/E compared to semiconductor industry median of 30x. The company's exclusive multi-product qualification for NVIDIA's Vera Rubin platform and high-margin HBM4 production create a competitive moat, though risks include supply normalization in 2027-2028 and geopolitical exposure to China.

items.title: Elon Musk Accused Of Building 'Most Management-Favorable' IPO Structure At SpaceX: Report
items.summary: Pension officials from New York and California have criticized SpaceX's proposed IPO governance structure as 'extremely' management-favorable, citing concerns over Musk's concentrated voting power through dual-class shares and his control over CEO removal. The officials urged SpaceX to adopt a one-vote, one-share structure and separate the Chair and CEO roles. SpaceX is targeting a $1.75 trillion valuation for its IPO.

items.title: 1 Incredible Bargain Hiding Right Under Investors' Noses
items.summary: Meta Platforms is presented as an undervalued investment opportunity despite strong 30%+ revenue growth. The article argues that while the market is concerned about Meta's high capital expenditures on AI and Reality Labs, the company's AI improvements to its advertising business are already delivering results. With a forward P/E ratio of less than 19 compared to the S&P 500's 21.9, Meta trades cheaper than the broader market despite superior growth. The author suggests Meta's upcoming AI superintelligence and smart glasses products could unlock additional value not yet priced into the stock.

items.title: Nvidia Reports Earnings This Month, and I'm Not Buying Shares. 1 AI Stock to Buy Now Instead.
items.summary: While Nvidia continues strong growth with 73% year-over-year revenue increase and guidance for 75% growth, the article argues its premium valuation (P/E of 46) leaves little room for error. Major customers like Google, Meta, and OpenAI are developing custom AI chips through Broadcom, reducing dependence on Nvidia. Amazon's custom chip business is highlighted as a better investment opportunity, growing at triple-digit pace with a $50 billion potential annual run rate, while trading at a more reasonable 32x earnings multiple.

items.title: Why Nebius Stock Jumped Today
items.summary: Nebius Group stock surged 15.88% after the AI cloud infrastructure provider raised its growth forecast, citing unprecedented demand for high-performance computing. The company is expanding into inference and agentic AI workloads through acquisitions, improving profitability with EBITDA reaching $129.5 million, and targeting over 4 gigawatts of power capacity by end of 2026 with plans for $7-9 billion in annual recurring revenue.

items.title: CoreWeave's Stock Is Plunging. Should You Buy the Dip?
items.summary: CoreWeave's stock fell over 10% in May after the company issued lighter-than-expected Q2 guidance and announced higher capital expenditures, despite revenue more than doubling year-over-year. While the company has a strong $100 billion backlog and partnerships with Meta, Nvidia, and Anthropic, concerns about rising losses, heavy debt, and margin pressure are weighing on investor sentiment. The article suggests high-risk investors may find value in the dip, while conservative investors should wait on the sidelines.

items.title: Which AI Supercycle Stock Will Make You Richer Over the Next 10 Years?
items.summary: AMD and Intel are both benefiting from the AI supercycle, but AMD emerges as the more dependable 10-year investment. AMD posted accelerating revenue growth of 38% YoY with data center revenue up 57%, strong customer commitments, and robust free cash flow. Intel shows turnaround signs with a 22% data center growth, but its foundry business is unprofitable and still needs to prove its manufacturing strategy can work at scale. AMD's growth is visible today while Intel's valuation already prices in future success.

items.title: Hagens Berman Files New Securities Class Action Against Super Micro Computer (SMCI) And Its Senior Executives: New Complaint Alleges Additional Corrective Disclosures; Lead Plaintiff Filing Deadline Remains May 26, 2026
items.summary: Hagens Berman filed a new securities class action lawsuit against Super Micro Computer and senior executives, alleging they concealed an illegal scheme to sell billions of dollars worth of advanced AI servers with Nvidia chips to China through a Southeast Asian shell entity, violating U.S. export restrictions. The lawsuit covers investors who purchased stock between February 2, 2024 and March 19, 2026. The fraud was revealed when a DOJ indictment was unsealed on March 19, 2026, charging Super Micro's co-founder with conspiracy to divert servers to China, generating approximately $2.5 billion in sales.

items.title: Tesla Stock Is Soaring. Is the $10 Trillion Robotaxi Story Finally a Buy?
items.summary: Tesla's stock has surged 26% over the past month, driven by excitement around its expanding Robotaxi autonomous ride-sharing service. However, the article questions whether the valuation is justified, noting that Robotaxi won't generate material revenue until 2027, while Tesla faces a $25 billion capital expenditure plan in 2026 and operates at single-digit margins. The author suggests waiting for a better entry price given the execution risks.

items.title: 'Tip Of The Iceberg': Culper Research Targets Nvidia's Alleged China Problem
items.summary: Culper Research released a short report alleging that Nvidia has a significant undisclosed 'China problem,' claiming over 20% of its FY 2026 compute revenues come from Chinese demand through illegal diversions via Southeast Asian intermediaries, particularly Singapore-based Megaspeed International. The report alleges Megaspeed is secretly financed by Alibaba and connects the operations to a DOJ indictment involving Super Micro Computer, warning Nvidia faces a massive revenue gap as China pivots to domestic alternatives.

items.title: Berkshire's Cash Pile Might Go To Data Centers, But On Its Own Terms
items.summary: Berkshire Hathaway has accumulated a record $380.2 billion cash position while remaining a net seller of stocks for 14 consecutive quarters. Under new CEO Greg Abel, the company is positioning itself to capitalize on AI infrastructure buildout through data centers, but on strict terms requiring hyperscalers like Meta, Google, and Microsoft to bear full costs of new generation and transmission infrastructure rather than socializing expenses across ordinary utility customers.

items.title: Trump Lands In China With $16 Trillion Of CEO Power — And One Big Ask For Xi
items.summary: President Trump traveled to China for high-stakes talks with President Xi Jinping, bringing a delegation of major U.S. tech CEOs with combined market cap of $16.4 trillion. Trump's primary objective is to request that China 'open up' its markets to American businesses, addressing ongoing disputes over tariffs, trade barriers, AI competition, and market access.

items.title: 3 Stocks You'll Regret Not Buying Right Now
items.summary: The article recommends three stocks as buying opportunities despite recent market rallies: Nvidia, positioned to benefit from continued AI infrastructure investment with potential 50% upside; Micron Technology, thriving from memory chip shortages with explosive revenue growth; and Meta Platforms, trading cheaply at 19x forward earnings with strong ad business growth driven by AI improvements.

items.title: Navitas Breaks Out on India Deal, Validating High-Power AI Pivot
items.summary: Navitas Semiconductor (NVTS) surged 24% on May 11 following a strategic partnership with Cyient to establish gallium nitride (GaN) manufacturing in India. The move validates the company's pivot from low-margin mobile chargers to high-growth AI data centers and EV infrastructure. With strong sequential revenue growth (18% Q4-to-Q1), a robust balance sheet ($221M cash, no debt), and dual GaN/SiC technology capabilities, Navitas is positioned as a key supplier for NVIDIA's next-generation AI platforms. The partnership also provides supply chain diversification and access to India's government-backed semiconductor initiatives.

items.title: Meet the AI Stock Crushing Palantir, Nvidia, and Alphabet Right Now
items.summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) has surged 115% year-to-date in 2026, significantly outperforming other AI stocks. The company reported strong Q1 earnings with 38% revenue growth and 57% data center segment growth, driven by accelerating demand for EPYC CPUs used in AI inference and agentic systems. CEO Lisa Su doubled the company's CPU market growth forecast to over 35% annualized through 2030, signaling a structural shift in the business. Despite trading at 35x 2027 earnings, AMD's PEG ratio of 0.69 suggests the stock remains undervalued relative to its 51% expected annualized earnings growth.

items.title: Inside Sam Altman's Dramatic Court Testimony: 'Complicated' Elon Musk Feelings, 'Hair-Raising' Tesla Talks And The Microsoft Temptation
items.summary: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman testified in court about his complicated relationship with Elon Musk, revealing concerns about Musk's attempts to gain control of OpenAI and pursue a Tesla merger. Altman stated he considered leaving OpenAI for Microsoft after his 2023 ouster, and described Musk's management style as unsuitable for running a research lab. The testimony is part of Musk's lawsuit alleging Altman and OpenAI looted the nonprofit structure through their Microsoft partnership.

items.title: SpaceX Could Become A $30 Trillion Monster, Ron Baron Says — And He Wants $1 Billion More
items.summary: Ron Baron, CEO of Baron Capital, plans to invest an additional $1 billion in SpaceX stock at its IPO, which could value the company at $1.5-1.75 trillion. Baron believes SpaceX could eventually be worth $10-30 trillion, driven by reusable rockets, Starlink, and space-based data centers. He also remains bullish on Tesla, expecting the stock could reach $2,000-2,500 per share within 10 years.

items.title: 3 Growth Stocks Long-Term Investors Should Buy in May
items.summary: The article recommends three growth stocks for long-term investors: Amazon, benefiting from accelerating AWS revenue and expanding chip and logistics businesses; Apple, leveraging its high-margin services ecosystem; and Dutch Bros, capitalizing on strong same-store sales growth and significant expansion opportunities in the restaurant sector.

items.title: The Q1 Cloud Face-off Is Over -- There Was 1 Clear Winner
items.summary: In Q1 2026, the Big Three cloud providers (Amazon, Microsoft, and Alphabet) all reported accelerating growth driven by AI demand. While AWS grew 28% to $37.6B and Azure grew 30% to $34.7B, Google Cloud emerged as the clear winner with 63% growth to $20B—its fastest pace since 2020. Google Cloud's AI revenue surged 800% year-over-year, and the company's backlog nearly doubled to $460B, making Alphabet a particularly compelling investment opportunity.

items.title: Pomerantz Law Firm Announces the Filing of a Class Action Against Babcock & Wilcox Enterprises, Inc.and Certain Officers – BW
items.summary: Pomerantz LLP has filed a class action lawsuit against Babcock & Wilcox Enterprises (BW) and certain officers for alleged securities fraud. The lawsuit claims defendants made materially false statements regarding a $2.4 billion power generation contract with Base Electron/Applied Digital, failing to disclose that BW's largest shareholder BRC had undisclosed ties to the counterparty and that the deal's true purpose may have been to provide exit liquidity for BRC rather than genuine business operations. The stock fell 11.59% following a short report by Wolfpack Research that exposed these relationships on March 12, 2026.

items.title: 3 Brilliant Growth Stocks to Buy Now and Hold for the Long Term
items.summary: The article highlights three growth stocks positioned for long-term transformation: Warby Parker is evolving into a holistic vision care platform with AI-powered smart glasses; Cava Group is expanding rapidly with strong unit economics and 38.9% digital revenue penetration; Dutch Bros is leveraging a 74% loyalty program participation rate and testing food offerings to drive growth beyond beverages.

items.title: Where Will Amazon Stock Be in 5 Years?
items.summary: Amazon demonstrates strong momentum across AWS, AI infrastructure, retail, and advertising segments. However, the stock's recent rally has raised valuation concerns. The key question is whether current free cash flow pressure represents a warning sign or a necessary investment to build a higher-margin AI platform.

items.title: Circle IPO Math Looks Richer After Arc Draws BlackRock and A16z
items.summary: Circle's stock surged 15% after securing $222M in presale funding for Arc, its new Layer-1 blockchain for institutional stablecoin settlement, valued at $3B. The round attracted major institutional investors including BlackRock, Andreessen Horowitz, and others. Circle's corporate valuation is anticipated around $4B-$5B ahead of its IPO. BlackRock's BUIDL fund, which has grown to $3.2B in AUM, relies on Circle's USDC smart contract for 24/7 redemptions, with Circle generating $150M in protocol fees from BUIDL integrations in 2025.

items.title: Hedge Fund Bets Big on Minerals Stock, According to Recent SEC Filing
items.summary: Long Corridor Asset Management disclosed a new position in Natural Resource Partners (NRP), purchasing 100,000 shares worth approximately $11.85 million in Q1 2026. The stake represents 3.70% of the fund's reportable assets. While NRP has shown strong 3-year performance (152% return, 36% CAGR) and significantly reduced debt, its valuation has expanded considerably with a P/S ratio of 7.7x, more than double its 10-year average, presenting mixed investment signals.

items.title: Alphabet vs. Microsoft: What Recent Revenue Trends Reveal
items.summary: Alphabet and Microsoft both demonstrate strong year-over-year revenue growth driven by AI investments. Alphabet maintains a larger revenue footprint ($109.9B vs $82.9B in Q1 2026) primarily from its dominant Google search engine (90% market share), while Microsoft focuses on cloud computing as its growth engine. Both companies are well-positioned for continued expansion as they integrate AI into their core businesses.

items.title: This Under-the-Radar Growth Stock Just Earned a Promotion
items.summary: Life360, a family safety app used on 1 in 7 U.S. smartphones, reported strong Q1 2026 earnings with 38% revenue growth—more than double its 17% user growth. The company's premium subscription business (Paying Circles) grew 27% year-over-year to 3 million accounts, while its newly highlighted advertising business quadrupled in revenue to $19.7 million following the Nativo acquisition. Despite margin compression from scaling operations, Life360 raised full-year 2026 guidance to 33-40% revenue growth.

items.title: The Next Phase of the AI Chip Supercycle Is Here. 1 Growth Stock Buy Before It Is Too Late (Hint: It Isn't Intel or Broadcom)
items.summary: Arm Holdings is positioned to become a major beneficiary of the shift toward AI inference workloads, which McKinsey estimates will dominate data center computing by 2030. The company's energy-efficient chip designs and new Arm AGI CPU for agentic AI have generated over $2 billion in customer demand. Management projects $15 billion in annual revenue from the AGI CPU by fiscal 2031 and overall revenue of $25 billion, with EPS reaching $9.00, suggesting potential 51% upside if valued at current tech multiples.

items.title: Massive News for Marvell Stock Investors: Nvidia Just Changed the Story
items.summary: Marvell Technology is strengthening its position in AI infrastructure through growth in custom silicon, rising data center revenue, and a strategic partnership with Nvidia. However, after a massive stock surge, investors face the question of whether the market has already priced in too much upside.

items.title: The Race to $10 Trillion: Nvidia vs. Alphabet
items.summary: Nvidia and Alphabet are competing to become the first company to reach a $10 trillion market valuation. Nvidia, currently valued at $5.2 trillion, leads as the world's largest company due to surging demand for its AI chips, with the upcoming Vera Rubin platform expected to drive further growth. Alphabet, valued at $4.8 trillion, is leveraging AI to enhance Google Search and Google Cloud while developing its own TPU chips. While Nvidia appears more likely to reach $10 trillion first based on current momentum and valuation multiples, Alphabet has a more sustainable long-term outlook due to its diversified business model and more stable P/E ratio.

items.title: Andy Jassy Just Said Something Big: Here's What It Means for Amazon's Stock
items.summary: Amazon CEO Andy Jassy called generative AI a 'once-in-a-generation opportunity' to justify the company's massive $200 billion capital expenditure forecast for 2026. While Jassy compares current AI investments to AWS's successful rollout in the 2000s, the article cautions that such heavy spending on data centers carries risk if anticipated demand doesn't materialize. Investors should await more tangible progress before getting excited about the strategy.

items.title: What AMD's Earnings Beat Could Mean for Intel
items.summary: AMD reported strong Q1 2026 earnings with 38% revenue growth and 57% data center segment growth, signaling robust AI chip demand. While the industry-wide growth benefits competitors like Intel, AMD is gaining market share in x86 chips. Intel has government backing but faces long-term competitive pressure from AMD and Nvidia.

items.title: OpenAI's IPO Dream Gets Massive Boost As Microsoft Reportedly Agrees To $38 Billion Revenue Cap— Nadella Says 'Very Proud' Of Early Bet
items.summary: Microsoft and OpenAI have renegotiated their partnership terms, capping total revenue-sharing payments at $38 billion. The deal allows OpenAI greater flexibility to work with other cloud providers like Amazon and Google while Microsoft remains the primary partner. Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella expressed pride in the company's early investment in OpenAI. These terms could strengthen OpenAI's appeal to investors ahead of a potential IPO by year-end.

items.title: Assessing Hawaiian Electric Industries (HE) Valuation After Maui Settlement And Higher Cost Guidance - Yahoo Finance
items.summary: Assessing Hawaiian Electric Industries (HE) Valuation After Maui Settlement And Higher Cost Guidance    Yahoo Finance

items.title: ROSEN, A GLOBAL AND LEADING LAW FIRM, Encourages Babcock & Wilcox Enterprises, Inc. Investors to Secure Counsel Before Important Deadline in Securities Class Action - BW
items.summary: A securities class action lawsuit has been filed against Babcock & Wilcox Enterprises, Inc. (BW) for allegedly making false and misleading statements regarding a Power Generation contract with Applied Digital. The lawsuit claims B&W's largest shareholder BRC had undisclosed conflicts of interest and that Applied Digital may not have actually needed the products/services, raising questions about revenue recognition. Investors who purchased B&W securities between November 5, 2025 and March 11, 2026 may be eligible for compensation, with a June 15, 2026 deadline to serve as lead plaintiff.

items.title: BABCOCK & WILCOX CLASS ACTION ALERT: Bragar Eagel & Squire, P.C. Urges Babcock & Wilcox Enterprises, Inc. Investors with Large Losses to Contact the Firm
items.summary: A class action lawsuit has been filed against Babcock & Wilcox Enterprises, Inc. (BW) for allegedly making false and misleading statements regarding a Power Generation Contract with Applied Digital. The lawsuit claims that B&W's largest shareholder BRC Group Holdings had undisclosed conflicts of interest and that Applied Digital may not have actually needed the products/services, raising questions about revenue recognition and overstated business prospects.

items.title: What Happens to Taiwan Semiconductor Stock if the AI Build-Out Slows Down? Here's My Answer.
items.summary: TSMC's stock has surged nearly 150% over the past year, driven by robust AI demand from hyperscalers. While Q1 2026 results showed strong 40.6% revenue growth and management raised full-year guidance, the stock's rich 35x P/E valuation leaves little room for error. The author warns that if AI infrastructure spending from a handful of major U.S. hyperscalers slows, TSMC could face significant headwinds, rating the stock a 'hold' rather than a 'buy' given the risks.

items.title: Why Sandisk (Not Micron) Could Be the Biggest Winner Of the AI Memory Era
items.summary: Both Sandisk and Micron have benefited from the AI memory boom, with Sandisk up 560% and Micron up 180% year-to-date. However, Sandisk emerges as the more compelling investment due to its multi-year customer contracts, debt-free balance sheet, $6 billion buyback program, and lower capital intensity compared to Micron's aggressive $25+ billion capex spending. While both stocks carry cyclical risk, Sandisk's business model offers better cycle protection.

items.title: Elon Musk Joins Trump's China Trip — Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang Not Invited
items.summary: Elon Musk will join President Trump on his China trip, but Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang was not invited. The White House is focusing on agriculture and commercial aviation deals, particularly Boeing aircraft orders. Huang's exclusion is notable given Nvidia's central role in U.S.-China tech negotiations and AI chip exports. The snub raises questions about where AI chip exports stand in broader U.S.-China negotiations.

items.title: Industries: Mag-7 Isn’t the Only AI Game in Town
items.summary: While the Magnificent-7 tech giants have dominated the AI trade since ChatGPT's launch, investor focus is broadening to semiconductor and component suppliers benefiting from massive AI infrastructure spending. The AI-11 semiconductor stocks have outperformed the Mag-7 this year, as hyperscalers' capital expenditure drives demand across the entire AI supply chain from foundries to memory chips to storage.

items.title: Cerebras’ $48 Billion IPO Tests the Market’s Inference Bet
items.summary: Cerebras Systems is going public with a $48 billion valuation at $150-$160 per share, up from an initial $115-$125 range, driven by 20x+ institutional oversubscription. The AI chip company's wafer-scale architecture targets inference workloads with major customers OpenAI and AWS validating its technology. However, the valuation at 51x trailing revenue carries risks including customer concentration, TSMC dependency, and Nvidia's competitive response.

items.title: After an 11% Drop, Is CoreWeave a Buy on the Dip or a Stock to Avoid?
items.summary: CoreWeave, a specialized AI compute provider, has dropped 11% following earnings despite doubling revenue to $2 billion. While the company boasts a $100 billion backlog and strong demand for GPU services, investor concerns about heavy debt reliance and increased capital spending forecasts ($31 billion) have pressured the stock. The article suggests CoreWeave could be attractive for aggressive investors betting on AI growth, but cautious investors may prefer established cloud leaders.

items.title: TSMC: The AI Infrastructure Monopoly Trading at a Discount
items.summary: TSMC trades at a 21x forward earnings multiple, a discount to semiconductor peers despite 30%+ revenue growth, 66% gross margins, and $95 billion in disclosed Nvidia purchase commitments. The company benefits from structural AI infrastructure demand with high visibility through long-term customer contracts, but geopolitical Taiwan concentration risk and historical cyclical foundry classification suppress valuation. Q2 2026 earnings in mid-July will be key to determining if the market reprices TSMC as infrastructure rather than cyclical manufacturer.

items.title: Elon Musk's One Move Turns 'Massive Losses' To 'Significant Profitability': Cathie Wood
items.summary: Cathie Wood highlights how xAI's strategic pivot to monetize its Colossus supercomputer infrastructure through partnerships with Anthropic could transform it from a massive cash burn into a profitable AI cloud utility. The shift from internal AI training expense to external revenue-generating service represents a new template for AI infrastructure economics, potentially turning it into a standalone profit engine rather than just a cost center.

items.title: Parabolic Semiconductor Rally Is Pricing in 2028 Already
items.summary: The semiconductor sector, led by the SOXX ETF which has surged 244% from April 2025 lows, is exhibiting parabolic characteristics that historically precede sharp reversals. While AI capex fundamentals are real, the rally is increasingly driven by speculative momentum in lower-quality names rather than fundamentals, with valuations already pricing in 2026-2028 earnings. Historical precedent suggests parabolic rallies unwind rapidly and painfully, with recovery taking years or decades.

items.title: This AI Stock Is Crushing Nvidia in 2026. It's Still a Buy After Soaring 240% This Year, According to Wall Street.
items.summary: DigitalOcean has surged 240% in 2026 by pivoting to AI services, significantly outperforming Nvidia's 15% gain. While Nvidia maintains dominance in AI infrastructure with 90% market share in accelerators and strong competitive moats, DigitalOcean's AI-Native Cloud platform targets the growing inference market. However, at 81x earnings, DigitalOcean appears expensive despite strong guidance, while Nvidia at 45x earnings with 53% annual earnings growth through 2028 offers better value for patient investors.

items.title: Palantir Just Showed Why Nvidia Stock Can Plunge on May 21
items.summary: Despite Palantir Technologies crushing earnings expectations with 85% revenue growth and raising guidance, its stock fell 8% due to lofty investor expectations and unsustainable valuation multiples. The article warns that Nvidia faces similar risks ahead of its May 20 earnings report, as history shows game-changing technologies experience bubble-bursting events when adoption expectations prove unrealistic. Additionally, internal competition from major customers developing cheaper alternative chips threatens Nvidia's pricing power.

items.title: 3 Top Stocks to Buy in May
items.summary: The article recommends three stocks for May 2026: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSMC), Amazon (AMZN), and Lemonade (LMND). TSMC benefits from strong AI chip demand with 41% YoY revenue growth and 66.2% gross margin. Amazon shows accelerated growth across AWS, e-commerce, and advertising, with AI driving triple-digit revenue growth. Lemonade, an AI-powered insurance disruptor, achieved 32% YoY IFP growth and is expected to reach profitability by year-end.

items.title: Where Will NuScale Power Stock Be in 5 Years?
items.summary: NuScale Power, a leading small modular reactor (SMR) company, is at a critical juncture with significant growth potential but substantial execution risks. While the company holds regulatory approval advantages and a major $10 trillion market opportunity, it faces mounting losses ($664M in 2025), severe shareholder dilution (1,500% share count increase since IPO), and repeated project delays. The company's first operational plant may not come online until 2030-2032, well behind original 2027 projections, leaving investors in a 'wait-and-see' mode regarding whether key catalysts will materialize.

items.title: Better Artificial Intelligence (AI) Stock: Alphabet or Microsoft
items.summary: The article compares Alphabet and Microsoft as AI hyperscalers investing heavily in cloud computing infrastructure. While Alphabet demonstrates stronger growth metrics (22% revenue growth vs. 18% for Microsoft) and advantages in cloud computing with its TPU chips, Microsoft emerges as the better investment due to significantly cheaper valuation. Alphabet trades at decade-high levels while Microsoft is near decade lows, making Microsoft the more attractive option despite Alphabet appearing as the healthier company.

items.title: Prediction: AI Infrastructure Stocks Will Crush the S&P 500 in 2026
items.summary: Tech giants are investing nearly $700 billion in AI infrastructure build-out in 2026 to meet current customer demand. As AI moves from research into real-world applications, infrastructure companies providing chips, servers, and networking equipment are positioned for explosive growth. With attractive valuations at seven-year lows and strong revenue momentum, AI infrastructure stocks are predicted to outperform the broader S&P 500.

items.title: 3 Growth Stocks Down 20% to Buy Right Now
items.summary: Despite record-high stock indexes, three growth stocks trading significantly below their all-time highs present potential buying opportunities. MercadoLibre faces margin pressures and loan defaults but maintains strong 49% revenue growth. Chewy has recovered operationally with 125% operating income surge despite being down 80% from peaks. Shopify's rapid 34% revenue growth and 88% operating income increase are overshadowed by AI-related concerns, though its ecosystem moat remains defensible.

items.title: Uber's $10 Billion Robotaxi Push Could Challenge Tesla And Waymo Dominance
items.summary: Uber is committing $10 billion to its robotaxi ambitions, allocating $2.5 billion for equity investments in robotaxi operators and $7.5 billion for fleet expansion starting in 2027, targeting over 100,000 vehicles. The company is partnering with multiple manufacturers including Lucid, Nuro, and others, leveraging NVIDIA technology. CEO Dara Khosrowshahi views the autonomous vehicle sector as a trillion-dollar market opportunity. Investor Gary Black predicts Uber could lead the global robotaxi sector over Tesla and Waymo due to its operational leverage and massive user base of 200 million monthly active customers.

items.title: A wealth advisor said goodbye to a tech ETF but not to the sector
items.summary: Resolute Wealth Strategies liquidated its entire position in JPMorgan U.S. Tech Leaders ETF (JTEK), selling 34,133 shares worth approximately $2.93 million in May 2026. However, the firm maintained its overall technology sector exposure through individual large-cap tech stocks and other sector-tilted ETFs, suggesting this was a strategic portfolio restructuring rather than a bearish tech call.

items.title: Brookfield’s Nuclear Venture And Legal Settlement Could Be A Game Changer For Brookfield (TSX:BN) - Yahoo Finance
items.summary: Brookfield’s Nuclear Venture And Legal Settlement Could Be A Game Changer For Brookfield (TSX:BN)    Yahoo Finance

items.title: Is CoreWeave Stock a Buy on the Dip as Revenue Continues to Skyrocket?
items.summary: CoreWeave's stock fell despite reporting 112% revenue growth to $2.08 billion and a nearly $100 billion project backlog. However, the company's adjusted EPS loss widened to $1.12 (worse than expected $0.91), Q2 guidance missed consensus, and rising component costs forced the company to increase its capex budget. CoreWeave remains highly leveraged and dependent on off-the-shelf GPUs, making it vulnerable to rising chip prices.

items.title: Why Caterpillar Could Be the AI Stock of the Year
items.summary: Caterpillar, a century-old construction equipment manufacturer, is emerging as a compelling AI infrastructure play due to massive demand for data center construction from tech giants like Amazon, Alphabet, and Microsoft. The company reported a 22% revenue increase to $17.4 billion in Q1 2026, record backlogs, and a 170% stock surge over 12 months, positioning it as an overlooked but critical player in the AI physical infrastructure build-out.

items.title: Amazon CEO Andy Jassy Has Good News and Bad News for Nvidia Investors
items.summary: Amazon is increasing AI chip purchases but shifting focus toward its own custom Trainium accelerators instead of Nvidia GPUs. CEO Andy Jassy revealed that AWS is deploying more Trainium chips than Nvidia chips and has a $225 billion backlog for Trainium compute. Other cloud providers like Google and Microsoft are similarly developing custom chips, threatening Nvidia's dominant market position in AI accelerators.

items.title: Jensen Huang Just Made a Major Announcement. Here's What It Means for Nvidia Investors.
items.summary: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang announced the creation of Nvidia Ising, the world's first family of open-source quantum AI models. Rather than building quantum computing hardware like competitors, Nvidia is positioning itself to control the software 'brains' of quantum systems. While quantum computing remains years away from commercial viability, this move demonstrates Nvidia's long-term strategic vision beyond its current AI GPU dominance.

items.title: Has Amazon Found the Next AWS?
items.summary: Amazon has launched Amazon Supply Chain Services (ASCS), opening its logistics network to external businesses similar to how AWS operates. The service, already adopted by major corporations like Procter & Gamble and 3M, could become a high-margin profit driver. While AWS remains Amazon's strongest business segment, ASCS represents another growth avenue, though its long-term success remains uncertain.

items.title: Missed April's AI Rally? These 3 Stocks Still Look Like Bargains.
items.summary: Despite April's AI sector rally, three megacap tech stocks remain attractive bargains. Microsoft trades 22% below its October high despite strong 18% revenue growth and 40% Azure growth. Nvidia, though near all-time highs, could see 40% upside as companies increase 2027 capital expenditures. Meta offers undervalued AI exposure with 33% revenue growth, trading below market multiples while growing faster than average.

items.title: Prediction: Amazon Will Outperform the S&P 500
items.summary: Amazon is positioned to outperform the S&P 500 in 2026 due to accelerating AWS cloud revenue growth (28% in Q1), expanding chip business ($20B run-rate), strong positioning in AI infrastructure, and underappreciated e-commerce efficiency gains through robotics and AI. The stock trades at an attractive 32x forward P/E compared to peers, offering room for growth and multiple expansion.

items.title: 3 Lessons for Transportation Industry Investors Following Spirit Airlines' Bankruptcy
items.summary: Spirit Airlines' bankruptcy provides three key lessons for transportation investors: avoid buying stocks immediately after bankruptcy emergence as companies often face recurring financial troubles, be cautious when struggling companies attempt major acquisitions as a last resort, and carefully monitor airline balance sheets and leverage ratios since high debt levels leave little room to weather adversity like fuel price spikes.
